summary: Matched the Big Blue stage function grBigBlue_801E61C4 by replacing its decomp stub with a small Ground_GObj update routine. The function pads the stack, calls grBigBlue_801EBAF8, then lb_800115F4 and Ground_801C2FE0. The PR also tightened the grBigBlue_801EBAF8 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void grBigBlue_801EBAF8(Ground_GObj*). Review noted the function was close but needed PAD_STACK(16) to match.
what_changed: Implemented grBigBlue_801E61C4 in src/melee/gr/grbigblue.c, replacing the prior decomp marker comment.
Added an include for lb/lb_00F9.h so grBigBlue_801E61C4 could call lb_800115F4.
The matched function now takes Ground_GObj* gobj, uses PAD_STACK(16), calls grBigBlue_801EBAF8(gobj), calls lb_800115F4(), and then calls Ground_801C2FE0(gobj).
Updated grBigBlue_801EBAF8 in src/melee/gr/grbigblue.h from an unknown signature to void grBigBlue_801EBAF8(Ground_GObj*).
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly scoped after being split from PR #2024, focusing only on the Big Blue grBigBlue_801E61C4 match.
Resolved the remaining mismatch with explicit PAD_STACK(16), matching reviewer guidance rather than overcomplicating the function body.
Improved type information by giving grBigBlue_801EBAF8 a concrete Ground_GObj* parameter and void return type.
Added the specific lb_00F9 header needed for lb_800115F4 instead of relying on an implicit or undeclared prototype.
decomp_lessons: Very small stage update functions may still require explicit stack padding to match, even when the call sequence is straightforward.
When matching a wrapper-like function, preserve the exact call ordering; here the sequence is grBigBlue_801EBAF8, lb_800115F4, then Ground_801C2FE0.
Matching one function can justify refining prototypes for nearby helper functions when the call site exposes the real argument type.
Builds with --require-protos are useful for catching missing includes and encouraging real function declarations.
naming_conventions: Stage functions in this file retain address-based names such as grBigBlue_801E61C4 and grBigBlue_801EBAF8.
Ground object parameters are named gobj when the function operates on a Ground_GObj*.
The refined helper prototype uses Ground_GObj* directly rather than UNK_PARAMS once call-site evidence is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16) to satisfy a remaining stack-layout mismatch.
Replaced a decomp marker with a direct call sequence rather than introducing unnecessary temporaries or control flow.
Pulled in lb/lb_00F9.h to support the lb_800115F4 call under prototype-required builds.
Reviewer evidence explicitly says the function did not seem to match yet but only needed stack padding.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Doesn't seem to match yet, but just needs some stack padding

follow_up_queries: Find other Big Blue stage functions still declared with UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS in grbigblue.h.
Search for other small ground stage update functions that call Ground_801C2FE0 and may need similar stack padding.
Check whether grBigBlue_801EBAF8 has since been fully matched and whether its refined prototype remained valid.
Look for other uses of lb_800115F4 in stage code to compare include and call-order patterns.
